+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: keyboard-navigation
+title: Promote nav.* commands to YAML and add Navigation top-level menu
+---
+## What
+
+Today the universal navigation commands (`nav.up`, `nav.down`, `nav.left`, `nav.right`, `nav.first`, `nav.last`, `nav.drillIn`, `nav.drillOut`) are defined as a frontend-only `NAV_COMMAND_SPEC` data table in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` (around line 28-70) ŌĆö they have no YAML stub, so they cannot appear in the native menu or be discovered by the command palette via the registry.
+
+Promote them to YAML:
+
+1. Create `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` with one entry per command:
+   - `id`: same id (`nav.up`, etc.)
+   - `name`: human label (`Navigate Up`, `Navigate Down`, `Navigate Left`, `Navigate Right`, `Navigate to First`, `Navigate to Last`, `Drill In`, `Drill Out`)
+   - `keys`: copy current bindings ŌĆö vim/cua/emacs blocks per spec. Keep `nav.drillIn` mapped to `Enter` and `nav.drillOut` mapped to `Escape` (these currently come from `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:104-114`).
+   - `menu`: `{ path: [Navigation], group: 0|1|2, order: 0..N }` so up/down/left/right are one group, first/last are a second group, drillIn/drillOut are a third group.
+   - `undoable: false` for all of them ŌĆö navigation does not mutate state.
+2. Make sure the new YAML file is picked up by the built-in command loader. The loader is `include_dir!`-based or build-time codegen (per memory: `dynamic-yaml-loading`). Verify by running the registry init and asserting all 8 ids resolve to a `CommandDef` with `menu` placement.
+3. Update `kanban-app/src/menu.rs:148-170` (`build_menu_from_commands`) to construct a `Navigation` submenu via `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), &mut menu_items)?` and append it to the menu bar between Edit and Window: `Menu::with_items(app, &[&app_menu, &file_menu, &edit_menu, &nav_menu, &window_menu])`.
+4. Keep the `execute` closures in `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` exactly as they are ŌĆö they read live `SpatialFocusActions` from a ref. The YAML stubs are pure metadata so the closure-bearing CommandDefs in app-shell remain the source of truth for execution. The frontend `useDispatchCommand` already merges YAML-defined and React-defined commands by id; verify by reading `command-scope.tsx`.
+5. Wire menu clicks: a click on a Navigation menu item must dispatch the same code path that the keybinding does today. In `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` look at how existing menu commands (e.g., `app.undo`) route their click ŌĆö likely via `dispatch_command(id)`. Make sure that path resolves the React-side execute when the closure lives only on the frontend (the existing pattern for similar frontend-resolved commands should already work; just verify with a manual code trace).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` exists and registers `nav.up`, `nav.down`, `nav.left`, `nav.right`, `nav.first`, `nav.last`, `nav.drillIn`, `nav.drillOut`.
+- [ ] All 8 nav commands appear under a `Navigation` submenu in the native menu bar, with their per-mode accelerators rendered (e.g., `Ōåæ` in CUA, `K` in vim).
+- [ ] All 8 nav commands appear in the command palette (`Mod+Shift+P`) with their `name` labels and key hints.
+- [ ] Pressing the keybinding (e.g., `j` in vim) still navigates as before ŌĆö no behavior regression.
+- [ ] `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` remains the executor; the closure logic is unchanged.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New Rust unit test in `swissarmyhammer-commands/tests/` (or extend an existing registry test): `nav_yaml_registers_all_eight_commands` ŌĆö load the registry, assert each id has a `CommandDef` with `menu.path == ["Navigation"]` and the expected `keys` per mode.
+- [ ] New Rust integration test for `kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: build the menu from a registry that has the new nav.yaml, assert the resulting menu contains a `Navigation` submenu with 8 items grouped correctly. Use the same test pattern as existing menu builder tests if any (search `menu.rs` callers in `kanban-app/tests/`).
+- [ ] Frontend test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.nav-commands.test.tsx`: mount AppShell, assert `globalCommands` includes all 8 nav ids and each has a non-null `execute`. Asserts no behavioral regression.
+- [ ] Run existing spatial-nav tests (`board-view.spatial-nav.test.tsx`, `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx`, `nav-bar.spatial-nav.test.tsx`) ŌĆö they must still pass.
+- [ ] Test command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` and `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test app-shell.nav-commands` ŌĆö all green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the YAML-registry test and the menu-builder test first; watch them fail; then create `nav.yaml`, update `menu.rs`, and re-run.
\ No newline at end of file

Promote them to YAML:

1. Create `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` with one entry per command:
   - `id`: same id (`nav.up`, etc.)
   - `name`: human label (`Navigate Up`, `Navigate Down`, `Navigate Left`, `Navigate Right`, `Navigate to First`, `Navigate to Last`, `Drill In`, `Drill Out`)
   - `keys`: copy current bindings ŌĆö vim/cua/emacs blocks per spec. Keep `nav.drillIn` mapped to `Enter` and `nav.drillOut` mapped to `Escape` (these currently come from `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:104-114`).
   - `menu`: `{ path: [Navigation], group: 0|1|2, order: 0..N }` so up/down/left/right are one group, first/last are a second group, drillIn/drillOut are a third group.
   - `undoable: false` for all of them ŌĆö navigation does not mutate state.
2. Make sure the new YAML file is picked up by the built-in command loader. The loader is `include_dir!`-based or build-time codegen (per memory: `dynamic-yaml-loading`). Verify by running the registry init and asserting all 8 ids resolve to a `CommandDef` with `menu` placement.
3. Update `kanban-app/src/menu.rs:148-170` (`build_menu_from_commands`) to construct a `Navigation` submenu via `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), &mut menu_items)?` and append it to the menu bar between Edit and Window: `Menu::with_items(app, &[&app_menu, &file_menu, &edit_menu, &nav_menu, &window_menu])`.
4. Keep the `execute` closures in `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` exactly as they are ŌĆö they read live `SpatialFocusActions` from a ref. The YAML stubs are pure metadata so the closure-bearing CommandDefs in app-shell remain the source of truth for execution. The frontend `useDispatchCommand` already merges YAML-defined and React-defined commands by id; verify by reading `command-scope.tsx`.
5. Wire menu clicks: a click on a Navigation menu item must dispatch the same code path that the keybinding does today. In `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` look at how existing menu commands (e.g., `app.undo`) route their click ŌĆö likely via `dispatch_command(id)`. Make sure that path resolves the React-side execute when the closure lives only on the frontend (the existing pattern for similar frontend-resolved commands should already work; just verify with a manual code trace).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` exists and registers `nav.up`, `nav.down`, `nav.left`, `nav.right`, `nav.first`, `nav.last`, `nav.drillIn`, `nav.drillOut`.
- [ ] All 8 nav commands appear under a `Navigation` submenu in the native menu bar, with their per-mode accelerators rendered (e.g., `Ōåæ` in CUA, `K` in vim).
- [ ] All 8 nav commands appear in the command palette (`Mod+Shift+P`) with their `name` labels and key hints.
- [ ] Pressing the keybinding (e.g., `j` in vim) still navigates as before ŌĆö no behavior regression.
- [ ] `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` remains the executor; the closure logic is unchanged.

Then add a Navigation top-level menu in the native menu bar.

-Promote them to YAML:
+Rationale: `swissarmyhammer-focus`'s own description in `Cargo.toml:25` is *"Spatial focus and keyboard navigation engine ŌĆö generic, no domain dependencies"*. The nav.* commands are the user-facing surface of that engine's kernel ops (`spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out`). Domain-free, kernel-coupled ŌĆö they belong with the focus crate, not with the generic `swissarmyhammer-commands` crate.
+
+This task depends on the linkme-based aggregator (prior task) so the focus crate can register itself without editing any other crate.

-1. Create `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` with one entry per command:
+### Steps
+
+1. **Add `include_dir` and `linkme` deps to `swissarmyhammer-focus/Cargo.toml`.** The crate description promises "no domain dependencies"; `include_dir` and `linkme` are infrastructure crates, not domain ŌĆö adding them is fine. Update the description if needed to keep it accurate.
+
+2. **Create `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`** with one entry per command:
    - `id`: same id (`nav.up`, etc.)
    - `name`: human label (`Navigate Up`, `Navigate Down`, `Navigate Left`, `Navigate Right`, `Navigate to First`, `Navigate to Last`, `Drill In`, `Drill Out`)
-   - `keys`: copy current bindings ŌĆö vim/cua/emacs blocks per spec. Keep `nav.drillIn` mapped to `Enter` and `nav.drillOut` mapped to `Escape` (these currently come from `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:104-114`).
-   - `menu`: `{ path: [Navigation], group: 0|1|2, order: 0..N }` so up/down/left/right are one group, first/last are a second group, drillIn/drillOut are a third group.
-   - `undoable: false` for all of them ŌĆö navigation does not mutate state.
-2. Make sure the new YAML file is picked up by the built-in command loader. The loader is `include_dir!`-based or build-time codegen (per memory: `dynamic-yaml-loading`). Verify by running the registry init and asserting all 8 ids resolve to a `CommandDef` with `menu` placement.
-3. Update `kanban-app/src/menu.rs:148-170` (`build_menu_from_commands`) to construct a `Navigation` submenu via `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), &mut menu_items)?` and append it to the menu bar between Edit and Window: `Menu::with_items(app, &[&app_menu, &file_menu, &edit_menu, &nav_menu, &window_menu])`.
-4. Keep the `execute` closures in `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` exactly as they are ŌĆö they read live `SpatialFocusActions` from a ref. The YAML stubs are pure metadata so the closure-bearing CommandDefs in app-shell remain the source of truth for execution. The frontend `useDispatchCommand` already merges YAML-defined and React-defined commands by id; verify by reading `command-scope.tsx`.
-5. Wire menu clicks: a click on a Navigation menu item must dispatch the same code path that the keybinding does today. In `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` look at how existing menu commands (e.g., `app.undo`) route their click ŌĆö likely via `dispatch_command(id)`. Make sure that path resolves the React-side execute when the closure lives only on the frontend (the existing pattern for similar frontend-resolved commands should already work; just verify with a manual code trace).
+   - `keys`: copy current bindings ŌĆö vim/cua/emacs blocks per `NAV_COMMAND_SPEC`. Keep `nav.drillIn` mapped to `Enter` and `nav.drillOut` mapped to `Escape` (currently in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:104-114`).
+   - `menu`: `{ path: [Navigation], group: 0|1|2, order: 0..N }` ŌĆö group 0 for up/down/left/right, group 1 for first/last, group 2 for drillIn/drillOut.
+   - `undoable: false` for all of them.
+
+3. **Wire the focus crate into the aggregator** by adding to `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` (mirroring the kanban pattern):
+
+   ```rust
+   static BUILTIN_COMMANDS: include_dir::Dir =
+       include_dir::include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/builtin/commands");
+
+   pub fn builtin_yaml_sources() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)> {
+       BUILTIN_COMMANDS
+           .files()
+           .filter(|f| f.path().extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) == Some("yaml"))
+           .filter(|f| f.path().parent() == Some(std::path::Path::new("")))
+           .filter_map(|f| {
+               let name = f.path().file_stem()?.to_str()?;
+               let content = f.contents_utf8()?;
+               Some((name, content))
+           })
+           .collect()
+   }
+
+   #[linkme::distributed_slice(swissarmyhammer_commands::BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS)]
+   static FOCUS_CONTRIBUTION: swissarmyhammer_commands::BuiltinCommandsContribution =
+       swissarmyhammer_commands::BuiltinCommandsContribution {
+           crate_name: "swissarmyhammer-focus",
+           priority: 100,  // between generic commands (0) and domain crates (200)
+           sources: builtin_yaml_sources,
+       };
+   ```
+
+   Note: this introduces a runtime dep from `swissarmyhammer-focus` to `swissarmyhammer-commands`. That's fine ŌĆö it's an infrastructure dep, not a domain one. The generic-commands crate doesn't import focus, so no cycle.
+
+4. **Update `kanban-app/src/menu.rs:148-170`** (`build_menu_from_commands`) to construct a `Navigation` submenu via `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), &mut menu_items)?` and append it between Edit and Window: `Menu::with_items(app, &[&app_menu, &file_menu, &edit_menu, &nav_menu, &window_menu])`.

+5. **Keep the React-side `execute` closures in `app-shell.tsx`'s `buildNavCommands` exactly as they are** ŌĆö they read live `SpatialFocusActions` from a ref. The YAML stubs are pure metadata (id, name, keys, menu); the closure-bearing CommandDefs in app-shell remain the source of truth for execution. The frontend `useDispatchCommand` already merges YAML-defined and React-defined commands by id.
+
+6. **Verify menu clicks for Navigation entries dispatch the React execute closure.** Trace the existing menu-click path for an existing frontend-resolved command (e.g., `app.about`) and ensure nav.* uses the same path.
+

+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` exists with all 8 nav.* entries.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus` exports `builtin_yaml_sources()` and registers a contribution at priority 100.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus` is in the binary's dep graph (kanban-app already depends on it transitively via state.rs); the slice element is reachable at runtime.
+- [ ] All 8 nav commands appear under a `Navigation` submenu in the native menu bar with their per-mode accelerators rendered.
